text.title: Feature Prioritization MoSCoW Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Feature Prioritization MoSCoW application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Items MUST be bucketed into exactly four buckets: Must / Should / Could / Won't. Extra buckets are rejected.
rule.rationale: Four-bucket model is the contract; adding 'Maybe' collapses Must into Should.

rule.statement: Must bucket MUST consume ≤60% of cycle capacity; over-cap is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without cap Must inflates until it equals total scope, defeating the purpose.

rule.statement: Won't bucket MUST contain ≥1 item per cycle and be actively used; empty Won't bucket is rejected.
rule.rationale: Won't is the boundary; an empty Won't means no decision was made.

rule.statement: A named tiebreaker rule MUST be cited (e.g. 'highest revenue impact wins ties'); ad-hoc tiebreaking is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without rule, ties resolve by loudest voice and prioritisation becomes politics.

rule.statement: Every Must item MUST carry a DoD + rollback plan; Must items without DoD are demoted to Should.
rule.rationale: Must means committed; committed without DoD is hope, not commitment.
